title: stylidium yilgarnense
intro: Stylidium yilgarnense, also known as the Western Club Spiked Stylidium, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used by the local Aboriginal people for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as soothing sore throats, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of skin irritations. The Western Club Spiked Stylidium is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, with the leaves and flowers being used in herbal preparations.
